architecture.scale_signals: high-concurrency customer analytics and multi-tenancy; scaling rapidly across products, customers, and acquisitions
architecture.latency_reliability: reliable ingestion; secure, scalable, multi-tenant customer reporting

success_metrics: One trusted semantic model powering all analytics and AI
Customer dashboards that scale independently and securely
AI-generated insights embedded directly into executive and customer reports
Fast, low-friction onboarding of new data sources and acquisitions
Clear governance, lineage, and documentation adopted across teams
implied_pain_points: create one trusted source of truth across products, customers, and acquisitions
make customer-facing analytics scale securely in a multi-tenant setup
enable AI summaries, narratives, and copilots only on governed data
accelerate onboarding of new products, data sources, and acquisitions
replace fragmented SQL Server warehouse patterns with a governed Microsoft-native analytics stack

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs direct evidence of designing governed analytics platforms, not just AI applications. The best proof would show production ownership of semantic layers, multi-tenant reporting, and AI outputs grounded in trusted data at real user scale.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: direct ownership of Databricks Unity Catalog production architecture
hands-on Power BI Semantic Models and Power BI Embedded at customer scale
leading acquisition onboarding of multiple data sources as a proven delivery record
building Microsoft Fabric-based copilots on governed data end to end
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps best to the governed AI part of the role because it shows an enterprise AI workflow platform with RAG, semantic caching, evaluation, guardrails, and 2,000 users.
Lantern maps to platform reliability and controlled access patterns through multi-provider routing and circuit breaker design.
Lead Engineer work in CQRS/event sourcing helps prove architecture discipline, but there is no direct Databricks, Power BI, or embedded analytics evidence in the candidate summary.

architecture.scale_signals: customer profile management and MDM solutions at scale

implied_pain_points: unify diverse source systems into a Guest 360 profile
deliver customer profile management and MDM at scale
apply governance, metadata, and catalog discipline across the enterprise
support compliance with data privacy regulations
align data architecture with business priorities, digital transformation, and regulatory requirements

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs direct proof of enterprise data architecture at 10+ years, especially Customer 360 or MDM delivery. It should also show governance, metadata/catalog work, CDC, graph database use, and a regulated or enterprise environment.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: owned a live Customer 360 or MDM program at airline scale
deep Neo4j or graph database delivery in production
direct Microsoft Fabric and AKS implementation ownership
TOGAF or SAFe certification if not explicitly held
led data privacy compliance architecture end to end
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 is the closest match because it shows governed enterprise AI workflows, but it is still not a direct Customer 360 or MDM story.
Lantern adds evidence of platform control, routing, and reliability, which helps for enterprise architecture but not for graph or CDC specifically.
The rest of the candidate summary is strongest on software architecture and distributed systems, so the fit is adjacent unless there is separate data architecture, MDM, or governance evidence.

architecture.scale_signals: high-volume data processing and real-time analytics
architecture.latency_reliability: robust, secure, resilient, optimized for scale-up; latency, throughput, and consistency tradeoffs

implied_pain_points: bridge business requirements and technical execution
design scalable pipelines and storage for high-volume and real-time analytics
modernize on-prem infrastructure to cloud-native or hybrid architectures
choose the right datastore for latency, throughput, and consistency needs
improve cost efficiency and query performance in existing systems

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 8-10 years in IT; at least 4 years in a Solution Architect or Lead Data Eng

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs hands-on solution architecture across data pipelines, cloud migration, and datastore decisions. It should show Python or Java, SQL, Docker or Kubernetes, and measurable improvements in performance, resilience, or cost.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: deep cloud-native data engineering at AWS, Azure, or GCP scale
expert-level Python or Java batch and stream processing ownership
production administration of PostgreSQL, MySQL, Cassandra, Snowflake, or BigQuery at scale
full on-prem to cloud-native migration ownership
broad multi-cloud native data services expertise without direct evidence
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Lead Engineer work in CQRS/event sourcing is the strongest match because it shows systems design, data flow discipline, and architecture thinking.
The microservices/RabbitMQ background supports distributed-system and integration credibility, which fits the bridge between business needs and technical execution.

Commander-4 and Lantern help with platform design and reliability, and the IoT or real-time WebRTC work can support scale/latency stories, but the candidate summary does not show a deep data-engineering stack.

implied_pain_points: translate business objectives into integrated digital solutions
set future-state architecture and modernization roadmaps
govern architecture across AI, data, cloud, and integration
evaluate emerging technologies and define reusable standards
align delivery teams to enterprise architecture direction

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 15+ years across enterprise architecture, solution design, application development, cloud platforms, middleware, integration, and data technologies

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs evidence of owning architecture across multiple domains, not just designing applications. It should show translating business goals into target-state platforms, governance and standards, plus shipping real AI/GenAI systems with RAG, routing, and reliability patterns.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Owning enterprise architecture governance across a whole enterprise without explicit review-board or blueprint ownership
Deep hands-on delivery of AWS, Azure, GCP, or OpenShift hybrid architectures if not directly built
Production fine-tuning, RAFT, or knowledge-graph work if only adjacent to the candidate's AI platform work
Claiming broad data-architecture mastery of Lakehouse, Data Mesh, or Data Fabric if not demonstrated
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps to AI/GenAI platform architecture, RAG, evaluation, guardrails, and reusable patterns
Lantern maps to multi-provider routing and resilient platform design
Lead Engineer CQRS/event-sourcing work maps to architecture patterns and modernization mindset

architecture.scale_signals: mission critical private cloud with over 800k cores; hundreds of Kubernetes clusters; OpenStack virtual machines; bare metal servers
architecture.latency_reliability: platform stability, resilience, observability; reliable, high-quality data and tooling; delivery and operational outcomes

implied_pain_points: scale and evolve compute-platform tooling across bare metal, virtual machines, and Kubernetes
improve inventory visibility and lifecycle management
raise reliability, observability, and operational excellence
balance hands-on technical leadership with people leadership
make data-driven decisions about platform health and delivery

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 3+ years as an Engineering Manager leading, coaching, and developing engineers with a mix of seniority and experience levels

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV must show people leadership plus hands-on platform engineering in compute or infrastructure environments. It should prove concrete improvements to reliability, observability, delivery, and lifecycle tooling, with metrics and examples of coaching engineers.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Leading a large infrastructure team with multiple direct reports if the candidate's experience is primarily technical leadership
Deep production Go work if not evidenced by code shipped in Go
Operating Kubernetes, OpenStack, or bare-metal infrastructure at 800k-core scale without direct experience
Owning OKRs, KPIs, or platform-health metrics without concrete numbers
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Lantern maps best to reliability and routing/circuit-breaker thinking for platform tooling
Commander-4 maps to platform ownership, quality, and operating an internal product at scale
Prior lead roles in CQRS/event-sourcing and microservices support hands-on system design and technical mentorship

make data-driven decisions about platform health and delivery

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV must show people leadership plus hands-on platform engineering in compute or infrastructure environments. It should prove concrete improvements to reliability, observability, delivery, and lifecycle tooling, with metrics and examples of coaching engineers.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Leading a large infrastructure team with multiple direct reports if the candidate's experience is primarily technical leadership
Deep production Go work if not evidenced by code shipped in Go
Operating Kubernetes, OpenStack, or bare-metal infrastructure at 800k-core scale without direct experience
Owning OKRs, KPIs, or platform-health metrics without concrete numbers
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Lantern maps best to reliability and routing/circuit-breaker thinking for platform tooling
Commander-4 maps to platform ownership, quality, and operating an internal product at scale
Prior lead roles in CQRS/event-sourcing and microservices support hands-on system design and technical mentorship

architecture.scale_signals: large-scale, complex distributed software systems; scaling the company

success_metrics: hire, lead, mentor, and manage engineers
conduct performance reviews and support career development
engineering objectives aligned with business goals
best practices for coding, security, testing, and performance optimization
implied_pain_points: Stand up and scale a new Agentic AI squad
Turn agentic AI capabilities into core product functionality across tools
Keep a SaaS product reliable, secure, and maintainable while moving quickly
Align engineering execution with business goals and product roadmap

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs proof of leading engineers and shipping an AI or SaaS platform with measurable reliability and quality discipline. It should also show concrete experience with agentic or orchestration-heavy systems, plus evidence that the candidate can set standards, mentor others, and keep delivery moving.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Formal people-management experience with hiring and performance reviews
Direct ownership of AML, anti-fraud, or banking products
Large-scale multi-agent program leadership as a people manager
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps strongly: enterprise AI workflow platform, 42 plugins, 2,000 users, RAG, semantic caching, evaluation, and guardrails show agentic-platform thinking and product-scale delivery.
Lantern maps well: LLM gateway with multi-provider routing and circuit breaker demonstrates orchestration and reliability engineering relevant to the role.
Technical Lead at Seven.One Entertainment Group adds senior engineering leadership context, but it does not prove formal team management.

success_metrics: design and delivery of large-scale, data-driven platforms
data governance, security, and privacy compliance
implied_pain_points: Design a GCP-centric data platform for a high-impact program
Integrate heterogeneous systems and live data sources without losing governance or security control
Support real-time streaming and possible GenAI use cases
Influence senior stakeholders on architecture and strategy

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 10–15 years in Data Engineering, Software Architecture, or Technology Consulting

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs proof of building and owning large-scale data platforms, especially with streaming and multi-source integration. It should also show executive stakeholder influence and concrete work on governance, security, and privacy-aware architecture.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Deep production ownership of the full GCP stack named in the JD
Formal executive-level data architecture leadership across large technical teams
Explicit data-governance and privacy compliance ownership
best_candidate_experience_mapping: IoT work with CoAP/MQTT is the closest fit for the role's live-data integration requirement.
Microservices/RabbitMQ and CQRS/event-sourcing map to the JD's heterogeneous, batch/event-driven architecture needs.
Commander-4 adds relevant AI-platform and evaluation/guardrail experience, but it does not prove GCP data-platform ownership.

architecture.scale_signals: mission critical private cloud with over 800k cores, hundreds of Kubernetes clusters, OpenStack virtual machines, and bare metal servers

success_metrics: improve visibility, efficiency, reliability, and lifecycle management
own the roadmap and delivery outcomes
maintain platform stability, resilience, and observability
foster operational excellence and engineering quality
implied_pain_points: Scale and evolve a mission-critical compute platform across bare metal, virtual machines, and Kubernetes
Improve inventory, tooling, and lifecycle management across a very large private cloud
Keep the platform reliable and observable while balancing delivery and operational outcomes
Lead a small cross-functional infra team without losing technical depth

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs proof of hands-on platform leadership across large-scale infrastructure with measurable reliability or operational improvements. It should also show the candidate can stay technical, lead a team, and own delivery in an environment where stability matters.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Deep Golang primary-language ownership
Hands-on Kubernetes, OpenStack, or bare-metal operations at 800k-core scale
Multiple direct reports in an infra organization
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Lantern maps best: multi-provider routing and circuit breaker work shows platform control, resilience, and operational thinking.
Commander-4 shows enterprise platform design, evaluation, and guardrails, which is relevant to owning tooling and reliability at scale.
Microservices/RabbitMQ and CQRS/event-sourcing demonstrate distributed-systems design, but the candidate does not have explicit infra-ops evidence for Kubernetes, OpenStack, or bare metal.

success_metrics: secure, scalable, cost-optimized cloud and data architectures
early identification of architectural risks and mitigation
mentoring delivery teams on cloud and data best practices
implied_pain_points: translate business use cases into secure, scalable GCP cloud and data architectures
bridge pre-sales with delivery and de-risk handoff
create migration and modernization roadmaps for apps and data
balance technical depth with commercial realities

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 10–16 years in cloud, data platforms, and customer-facing architecture

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs to show end-to-end architecture ownership across cloud and data platforms, ideally with customer-facing solutioning or pre-sales exposure. The best evidence is quantified modernization work that improved scale, cost, and reliability, plus examples of partnering with sales or delivery teams.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Do not claim deep GCP or BigQuery ownership unless it is directly evidenced.
Do not claim multi-cloud solution leadership unless the CV shows it.
Do not overstate executive pre-sales ownership or delivery handover scope without proof.
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 maps to platform architecture, RAG, evaluation, and governance.
Lantern maps to routing, gateway-style architecture, and reliability-oriented design.
Lead Engineer work in CQRS/event-sourcing and microservices maps to distributed system architecture and modernization.

success_metrics: internal product innovation and platform iteration
seamless transition from solution design to implementation and delivery
implied_pain_points: turn complex customer requirements into reusable AI agent solutions
convert lighthouse projects into scalable industry playbooks
bridge market needs and R&D iteration
support bid responses and delivery handoff

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 3+ years in enterprise-grade AI platforms, AI Agents, or LLM solutions

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: The CV should prove hands-on enterprise AI platform work that translated into reusable customer-facing solutions, with demos or POCs and a clear path from requirements to delivery. The strongest evidence is building an AI platform, turning one-off customer asks into repeatable patterns, and working across product and technical stakeholders.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Do not imply Mandarin or Arabic fluency unless the CV states it.
Do not claim direct sales ownership or quota responsibility.
Do not claim experience with HiAgent, Coze, Dify, or n8n unless directly shown.
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 is the closest fit because it is an enterprise AI workflow platform with plugins, RAG, guardrails, and evaluation.
Lantern maps to gateway/routing and platform integration work that supports AI solution design.
Any demo or POC work on those platforms maps well to customer-facing solutioning and product iteration.

success_metrics: hire and manage a strong engineering team
support performance reviews and career development
performance, reliability, and maintainability of the SaaS product
engineering objectives aligned with business goals
effective policies, standards, and procedures for the team
implied_pain_points: stand up and scale a new Agentic AI squad
keep a SaaS product performant and reliable while adding Agentic AI capabilities
create a high-performing engineering culture with strong hiring and performance management
align technical execution with product and business goals

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 12+ years of software engineering experience; 5+ years managing teams

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: The CV needs evidence of leading engineers, hiring or performance management, and shipping reliable distributed systems at scale. It should show that the candidate can translate product goals into team execution while keeping quality, security, and performance under control.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: Do not claim formal people management, performance review ownership, or hiring unless the CV explicitly shows it.
Do not overstate AML, Fraud, or Banking domain depth.
Do not claim prior ownership of LLM orchestration frameworks or multi-agent architecture unless directly evidenced.
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Technical Lead at Seven.One Entertainment Group maps to technical leadership and cross-functional execution.
Commander-4 maps to owning an enterprise AI platform with architecture, guardrails, evaluation, and product discipline.
Lantern and earlier distributed-systems work map to reliability, routing, and large-scale system ownership.

hard_skills_must_have: AI and Machine Learning tools and platforms
success_metrics: successful deployment of scalable and efficient pricing solutions
implied_pain_points: needs AI-powered pricing strategy and systems
needs robust technical design and scalable deployment
needs cross-functional coordination to satisfy client requirements
needs mentoring and technical leadership for the team

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV would show that you have led technical design and delivery for production AI systems, not just prototypes. It should quantify scale, reliability, and deployment outcomes, and ideally show adjacent analytics or optimization work if direct pricing experience is limited.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: deep pricing-analytics domain ownership
hands-on AWS/GCP/Azure pricing-system delivery if not actually done
leading a pricing team or owning budget
supply-chain or retail pricing expertise
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4: closest match for production AI platform design, RAG, evaluation, guardrails, and scaling to 2,000 users.
Lantern: relevant for robust multi-provider AI integration and circuit-breaker style reliability.
Technical Lead at Seven.One: evidence of technical leadership, design oversight, and mentoring in a live product environment.

architecture.scale_signals: large-scale creative and marketing operations transformations

implied_pain_points: enterprise customers need help applying GenAI to creative and marketing workflows
business scenarios must be translated into Adobe solutions and requirements
customer issues must be mitigated before they affect program success
the org needs scalable frameworks for integrating first- and third-party AI into existing workflows
adoption and value realization must be demonstrated

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV would need to show enterprise GenAI delivery, customer-facing solution design, and measurable adoption or value realization. Evidence should include workshops, executive communication, POCs, and integration work with first- and third-party models.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: deep Adobe-specific GenAI transformation leadership
direct ownership of C-suite customer programs at enterprise scale
5+ years of SaaS solution environment if not explicitly proven
hands-on ownership of first-party/third-party model strategy beyond platform integration
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4: strongest analog for enterprise AI workflow enablement, evaluation, guardrails, and turning AI capabilities into usable product workflows.
Lantern: relevant for multi-provider routing and resilient AI integration patterns across models.
Technical Lead at Seven.One: shows technical leadership and cross-functional delivery, but the CV must still prove customer-facing engagement work to be competitive.

success_metrics: curricula aligned with UK qualification frameworks and Saudi accreditation standards
implementation of Internal Quality Assurance Cell (IQAC)
monitor student progression, retention, and graduation rates
capstone projects tackle real-world AI/ML problems
high-quality learning content and teaching standards
implied_pain_points: academy needs modern AI/ML curriculum design
faculty performance and quality assurance need stronger structure
student outcomes and progression need improvement
accreditation alignment and UK partnership management are core challenges
the institution needs industry partnerships and research visibility

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 10+ years academic experience; 3-5 years as HoD, Dean, or Principal

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV would need a PhD, academic leadership, curriculum and accreditation ownership, and faculty management experience. Technical depth alone is not enough unless it is paired with higher-education governance and measurable student outcome results.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: PhD in CS, AI, or ML
served as HoD, Dean, or Principal
ownership of UK higher-education accreditation and QA
faculty recruitment/appraisal leadership
publication record in reputable journals
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Technical Lead at Seven.One: demonstrates mentoring and technical leadership, but only loosely maps to faculty leadership.
Commander-4: shows evaluation rigor, guardrails, and structured platform delivery, which is the closest technical analog to quality assurance.
Lead Engineer and backend roles: show breadth and delivery, but they do not substitute for academic administration, accreditation, or the required PhD.

success_metrics: curricula aligned with UK qualification frameworks and Saudi accreditation standards
student progression, retention, and graduation rates
internal quality assurance for academic delivery
partnerships with tech giants and local industry leaders
faculty and student publication in reputable journals
implied_pain_points: bridge advanced technical education with academic administration
keep AI/ML and Computer Science curricula industry-relevant
maintain accreditation and academic quality across programs
build and sustain a high-performing faculty organization
improve student outcomes and intervention for at-risk students
grow research and industry partnerships

disqualifiers.requires_specific_domain_years: 10+ years of academic experience; 3-5 years as Head of Department, Dean, or Principal

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs to show real academic leadership: curriculum design, faculty hiring and appraisal, accreditation work, and measurable student outcomes. It also needs direct evidence of UK higher-education familiarity and the ability to evaluate code, technical projects, and AI models without relying on generic management language.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: direct academic dean or department-head experience
accreditation leadership in UK higher education
faculty performance management at a university or academy
student outcomes ownership such as retention or graduation rates
publication or research leadership
PhD-level subject authority
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 is the best adjacent evidence because it proves AI/ML platform judgment, evaluation, guardrails, and quality-focused leadership, which maps to assessing technical curriculum and technical work.
Lantern maps to architecture and resilient multi-provider AI systems, but it is still product/platform experience rather than academic administration.

Technical Lead at Seven.One and earlier lead/backend roles can support leadership, mentoring, and cross-functional delivery, but they do not directly prove accreditation, faculty management, or student-success ownership.
